card_summary: A single-page clipping from Le Devoir (Montreal, Quebec, Canada), a major French-language daily newspaper, dated Saturday, December 13, 1975, page 14. Given the date and source, this is likely a French-language article or review related to Philip K. Dick or his work during the mid-1970s, a period of growing international recognition for the author.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from Le Devoir (Montreal, Quebec, Canada), dated Saturday, December 13, 1975, page 14. Le Devoir is a major French-language daily newspaper and one of Quebec's most respected publications. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 15, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

The December 1975 date places this during a significant period in Dick's international reception. Dick had always been more widely appreciated in francophone culture than in the Anglophone literary mainstream, with French publishers translating his work extensively and French critics treating him as a serious literary figure years before similar recognition came in the United States. A 1975 article in Le Devoir may relate to the growing French-Canadian interest in Dick's work or could document coverage of his fiction in the broader francophone literary world.
